title: Bond Prices and Yields: A bond pays a coupon of 60 annually and has...
summary: Calculate the yield to maturity of a bond from its coupon, price, and maturity.
prompt: A bond pays a coupon of $60 annually and has a current market price of $800. If it matures in 10 years, what is the yield to maturity (YTM)?
solution:
Step 1: Identify Key Variables
Annual coupon payment (C) = $60, Market price (P) = $800, Years to maturity (n) = 10

Step 2: Use YTM Formula
YTM = [C + (F - P) / n] / [(F + P) / 2]

Step 3: Calculate YTM
Assuming a face value (F) of $1,000: YTM = [60 + (1,000 - 800) / 10] / [(1,000 + 800) / 2]
YTM = [60 + 20] / 900 = 0.0889 or 8.89%
